subject: [PATCH v5 0/6] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: The reviewer questioned the clarity of a specific dereference in the code, also expressing confusion about the naming and location of a documentation file related to io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy.
reviews.raw_body:
This dereference is not clear to me.

The name of the suggested doc is even more puzzling for me
io-uring.rst is really not an adequest standalone name for this doc
and fuse/uapi path is unconventional in Documentation.
The doc is not even pure uapi, it explains about buffer pools and zero copy.

Why not include the details inline in fuse-io-uring.rst?
It's not going to be a huge doc and all details seem to be intertwined.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about documentation naming and clarity, specifically the name of the doc and how to make dereferencing clearer, and is open to suggestions.
reviews.raw_body:
The details were originally in fuse-io-uring.rst but I think Miklos
preferred having a separate fuse/uapi directory [1] for how userspace
interacts with it.

I'm happy to change the name of the suggested doc - is there an
alternative name you'd prefer more? How do you suggest making the
dereference clearer?

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/fuse-devel/CAJfpegt8sfO681aoixfCc2XvUP-OH50GVHG-vmF8Q3oGqxF9MA@mail.gmail.com/#t

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy to the FUSE file system. The current implementation tightly couples entries and buffers, requiring a dedicated payload buffer for each entry. This patch decouples them by allowing servers to pass in a buffer pool that the kernel can use as needed. Additionally, it introduces zero-copy functionality, which allows servers to directly access client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer. The benefits of this patch include reduced memory usage requirements and improved throughput, particularly when backing I/O is not the bottleneck.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the buffer pool overview should not be moved to the uAPI document, but rather an additional uAPI document is needed

Reviewer suggested moving documentation of commands and structs from fuse-uapi.rst to either a more descriptive file name or the existing design doc in fuse-io-uring.rst, while keeping the overview of buffer pools and zero copy in the latter.
reviews.raw_body:
My reading of this comment is that we ALSO need a uAPI document
not move the buffer pool overview into the uAPI document.

---

Either document commands and structs in fuse-uapi.rst
(as a placeholder for more commands documentation) or in
fuse-uapi-io-uring.rst.
can be under fuse/uapi/ if this feels more organized.
My point is that doc names can be self describing and I find it better this way.

But I don't think that the overview of buffer pools and zero copy belongs there.
I think they are still very much useful in the fuse-io-uring.rst design doc
as in v4 and I do not think that Miklos objected to this point.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy to the FUSE file system. The current implementation tightly couples entries and buffers, requiring a dedicated payload buffer for each entry. This patch decouples them by allowing servers to pass in a buffer pool that the kernel can use as needed. Additionally, it introduces zero-copy functionality, which allows servers to directly access client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer. The benefits of this patch include reduced memory usage requirements and improved throughput, particularly when backing I/O is not the bottleneck.
